questions.text: I detect when someone is saying yes but meaning no.
I hold the view that experiencing emotions at a moderate intensity gives me a kind of clarity.
I believe the world would be easier to navigate if people just said what they meant.
I do not tend to analyze social interactions after they happen.
I feel that consistency is what transforms average effort into exceptional outcomes.
I feel that the moral pull of suffering is the experience that most forcefully reminds me of what I owe to others.
I prefer to be the architect of a project rather than a contributor.
I hold the view that my steady disposition helps me think clearly when it matters most.
I feel that flat, collaborative structures produce better results.
I hold the view that a structured, rational framework for engaging with human pain is not a substitute for compassion but a complement that makes compassion more productive.
I am attracted to opportunities where I can blend into a well-functioning team.
I find that keeping my personal goals front and center gives my life direction.
I tend to understate my own needs so as not to divert attention from others.
I find that my ability to separate emotional content from factual content during human crises is what makes my help durable and reliable.
I make many of my decisions based on what feels right rather than on careful analysis.
I focus on what's interesting and energizing right now rather than optimizing for the long term.
I feel that my own dreams and aspirations are worth fighting for.
I find that my emotional swings cover an enormous range — from ecstatic to devastated in the same day.
